metadata.greeting: Hello! This weekend is bursting with exciting activities for your 3-year-old in Hackney and beyond. I've found a wonderful mix of local events and some that are worth a little journey, like the Paddington Bear Experience and exploring the animals at ZSL London Zoo.

activities.title: Tiny Planet
activities.address: 43 White Horse Road, Limehouse, London E1 0ND
activities.description: A children's theatre show.
activities.whyItWorks: A shorter theatre experience for slightly older toddlers, but still engaging.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Limehouse station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for age appropriateness closer to the date.

activities.title: The Together Space March Family Event
activities.description: A World Book Day-themed event with cooking workshops, collage table, weightlifting, book swaps, sensory space, active play area, soft play and an inflatable activity run.
activities.whyItWorks: Variety of activities to keep a 3-year-old entertained, sensory and active play options.

transport.fromHackney: Location varies within Hackney, check the specific location for transport options.
transport.tip: Check the exact location and plan travel in advance.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddler-friendly design, free, and located within the expansive Olympic Park for additional exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk into the park.
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: A museum designed for children, with exhibits focused on creativity, play and design.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, plenty of space to move, and tailored to young children's interests.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Discover Children’s Story Centre
activities.description: An indoor/outdoor play space designed to inspire imagination and storytelling with a musical forest, spaceship, clouds, craft area and story garden.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for young children, encourages active play and imagination, has an on-site cafe.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with animals and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, free entry, and easy to reach.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times to get the most out of your visit.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A community-focused farm in the heart of Hackney with animals, gardens, and educational activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Conveniently located, free, provides a hands-on experience with animals and nature.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney, easily accessible by bus or walking.
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops on their website.

activities.title: East Ham Nature Reserve
activities.description: A nature reserve with woodland and meadows, perfect for a nature walk.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration, free, and good for observing nature.

transport.fromHackney: Take the District Line to East Ham, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Wear appropriate footwear for walking.

activities.title: The Paddington Bear Experience
activities.description: A lavish central London experience that is a homage to the iconic family films.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive and charming, brings a beloved character to life.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then the Underground (Central Line) to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: ZSL London Zoo
activities.description: A famous zoo with a wide variety of animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, and educational.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston, then a bus to the Zoo.
transport.tip: Check feeding times and special events on the website.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A large playground inspired by Peter Pan with teepees, a pirate ship, and sensory trails.
activities.whyItWorks: Large, varied play areas, free, and located in a beautiful park.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Notting Hill Gate, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Can get crowded, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Interactive exhibits exploring science and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: Many hands-on exhibits suitable for toddlers, free entry, and engaging for all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and temporary exhibitions.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Dinosaurs, animals, and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Fascinating exhibits for children, free entry, and impressive architecture.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book a free timed entry ticket in advance.

activities.title: Jubilee Gardens Play Area
activities.description: A playground located near the London Eye, with engaging play areas.
activities.whyItWorks: Conveniently located near a major attraction, offers a fun break for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then the Underground (Central Line) to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Combine with a ride on the London Eye or a walk along the South Bank.

activities.title: St James's Park Playground
activities.description: A playground in a beautiful park near Buckingham Palace.
activities.whyItWorks: Scenic location, playground, and opportunities for bird and critter watching.

transport.fromHackney: Take the District Line to St James's Park.
transport.tip: Combine with watching the Changing of the Guard ceremony.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages, while you explore the decks and keep your eyes peeled for the hidden eggs. Each egg has a letter on it — find them all and unscramble a secret nautical code. Cracked it? Head to the Cutty Sark café located beneath the hull to claim a free chocolatey treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive, educational, and rewarding with a chocolatey treat.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark station
transport.tip: Best suited for children over 5

activities.title: Kish Kush
activities.description: Kish Kush follows two strangers meeting for the first time, separated by a paper wall. They speak...
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive performance, and suitable for the age range

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Clapham Junction, then train to Wimbledon
transport.tip: Check website for exact times and booking

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: Stage adaptation of Julia Donaldson's story brought to life with rhyme, original music and delightfully expressive puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets, music, and familiar story.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington, then bus to near the theatre.
transport.tip: Check website for show times and booking.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive, involves personal items, and includes playtime.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Underground (Northern Line) to London Bridge.
transport.tip: Bring a favorite toy!

activities.title: We're Going On A Bear Hunt
activities.description: A stage adaptation of the classic children's book.
activities.whyItWorks: A well-known and loved story, likely to be engaging for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Clapham Junction, then train to Wimbledon.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as popular show.

activities.title: Disney on Ice: Into the Magic
activities.description: Be inspired by Disney's Magical characters.
activities.whyItWorks: Disney characters, ice skating, and a visually stimulating show.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Finsbury Park, then Piccadilly Line to Wembley Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance for best seats.

activities.title: Fairy Tales
activities.description: An interactive exhibition exploring enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, through books, costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive, tactile, and gently stimulating, brings fairy tales to life.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston/St Pancras.
transport.tip: Go early in the day – the grounds are calmer before lunch.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Kids are invited to explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animals with an Easter egg hunt, encouraging exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston, then a bus to the Zoo.
transport.tip: Book zoo tickets in advance online.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: Watch Simba's adventures on the Serengeti come to life in The Lion King at Lyceum Theatre, told through stirring puppetry and live music.
activities.whyItWorks: Stirring puppetry and live music

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then the Central line to Holborn
transport.tip: Aisle seats recommended
